title: Operator Fail-Closed Boundary
non_product_work: Resolve the bootstrap gap where fail-closed routing can escalate derivable operational drift to the operator instead of using Codex PM or CLI-owned mechanical repair.
origin: The 2026-05-26 PRD-002 research review warned that pervasive fail-closed behavior can manufacture operator toil when ordinary registry, ledger, optional-field, projection, malformed-artifact, or workflow bookkeeping drift is escalated instead of repaired mechanically. The operator confirmed that operator-blocking fail-closed behavior should be reserved for safety, product, UAT, policy, business, cost, irreversible-risk, and genuinely ambiguous scope gates. Derivable operational drift should route to CLI-owned mechanical repair when approved repo artifacts determine the intended state, with expected-current-state checks, immutable transition history, and no authority to invent scope, grant approvals, override policy, resolve ambiguous cost/risk, perform irreversible operational actions, or force-resolve unsafe claim recovery.
scope: Define a repo-native Operator Fail-Closed Boundary contract that distinguishes Operator-Blocking Gates from Codex-Owned Technical Decisions and Operational Drift.

Name the operator-owned gate families: product direction, UAT approval or stale UAT, policy change, business tradeoff, explicit cost or risk approval, irreversible operational-risk approval, safety-critical release authorization, and genuinely ambiguous scope.

Name derivable operational drift families that should route to Codex PM or CLI-owned mechanical repair when approved artifacts determine the intended state, including missing derivable metadata, malformed supported artifacts, registry or ledger drift, projection mismatch, current-context or roadmap bookkeeping drift, and workflow status disagreement.

Define required evidence for mechanical repair: approved source artifacts, observed current state or report hash, expected-current-state check, exact intended repair, atomic apply-or-no-change behavior where the current CLI supports it, immutable transition or lifecycle evidence, and one recompute or validation pass after repair.

Define repair-overreach refusals: a mechanical repair path must halt for operator input if it would invent product scope, grant UAT or product approval, override policy, resolve ambiguous cost or risk, approve spend, perform irreversible operational action, force-resolve unsafe claim recovery, break dependencies, or change merge/push/deploy authority.

Define operator-escalation-overuse smells and required disposition evidence so future Stage 0, Stage 1, cockpit status, claimability, landing, and validation flows do not ask the operator to resolve routine technical routing or derivable operational drift.
Add a CLI-readable policy or template plus validation that verifies operator-boundary definitions, mechanical-repair evidence fields, repair-overreach refusals, and smell-trigger alignment.

Add focused tests proving missing operator-boundary fields fail closed, derivable operational drift routed to the operator is rejected, repair-overreach attempts are rejected, and a complete operator-boundary policy is accepted.

Preserve existing AGENTS.md and CONTEXT.md authority: Codex PM owns routine technical routing, implementation mechanics, skill/tool selection, review depth, test strategy, artifact structure, and Git plumbing decisions unless an operator-owned gate is crossed.
Keep the repair limited to operator-boundary contracts, templates, policy or validator code, smell-trigger alignment, focused tests, and necessary roadmap/context/gap-ledger evidence.
Record CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence in Stage 1; CLEAN_CODE.md was read on 2026-05-28 before creating this brief, and clean-code compliance must be evaluated before landing.

Stage capability scope: Codex PM owns technical routing; Test Writer owns RED evidence; Writer may edit operator-boundary templates, policy parsing, validation, command wiring, cockpit/status required-operator-input derivation if needed, and focused tests; CodeRabbit and Local Qwen own Stage 4 review evidence; Landing Agent owns Stage 5 verdict/action evidence.

Operator-blocking boundary for this chore: no operator-owned input is required unless implementation would change product direction, UAT policy, workflow policy beyond defining the already queued operator fail-closed boundary gap, business tradeoffs, explicit cost/risk posture, external service setup, paid reviewer spend approval, paid reviewer routing, live routing, scheduler authority, claim/worktree authority, installed global skill contents, merge/push/deploy authority, or broader workflow scope.

Future-work scope: this chore must not implement Work Intake Ledger, Work Intake Triage Skill, Operator Inbox notification flow, Repo PM Coordinator protocol, claimability reports, claim leases, CAS claim authority, fencing tokens, idempotency-key enforcement, work-surface reservations, Git Mutation Serializer behavior, worktree lifecycle, scheduler execution, event-driven wakeups, observability traces, Stage Capability Scope, Token-Cost Failsafe, Evidence Freshness SLOs, local server/API mode, cockpit UI implementation, PR/CI workflow execution, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, or unrelated Phase 8 work.
acceptance_criteria: The chore brief exists at docs/work/<ID>/brief.md and links to BANDIT-GAP-OPERATOR-FAIL-CLOSED-BOUNDARY.

An Operator Fail-Closed Boundary template or policy artifact names Operator-Blocking Gates, Codex-Owned Technical Decisions, Operational Drift, CLI-Owned Mechanical Repair, repair-overreach refusals, operator-escalation-overuse smells, required evidence, and source artifacts.

Validation fails closed when operator-owned gate definitions omit product direction, UAT, policy, business, explicit cost/risk, irreversible operational-risk, safety-critical release authorization, or genuinely ambiguous scope boundaries.
Validation fails closed when derivable operational drift is routed to the operator even though approved repo artifacts determine the intended state and no operator-owned gate is missing.

Validation fails closed when a mechanical repair path can invent product scope, grant UAT or product approval, override policy, resolve ambiguous cost/risk, approve spend, perform irreversible operational actions, force-resolve unsafe claim recovery, break dependencies, or change merge/push/deploy authority.

Validation requires mechanical repair contracts to name approved source artifacts, observed current state or report hash, expected-current-state check, exact intended repair, immutable transition or lifecycle evidence, and post-repair validation or recompute behavior.

Validation confirms smell-trigger policy covers missing operator-owned input, operator escalation overuse for derivable drift, technical-question over-delegation, and mechanical repair overreach with required evidence fields and correct escalation targets.

Focused tests prove operator-boundary validation rejects missing gate families, missing repair evidence fields, operator escalation for derivable drift, and repair overreach, and accepts a complete low-risk operator-boundary policy.

If cockpit status or derived reports expose required operator input, they distinguish operator-owned input from operational drift and cite source artifacts without becoming canonical workflow authority.

The implementation does not implement Work Intake Ledger, Work Intake Triage Skill, Operator Inbox notification flow, Repo PM Coordinator protocol, claimability reports, CAS claim operations, claim leases, fencing tokens, idempotency-key enforcement, work-surface reservations, Git Mutation Serializer behavior, worktree bootstrap contracts, scheduler execution, event-driven wakeups, observability traces, Stage Capability Scope, Token-Cost Failsafe, Evidence Freshness SLOs, local server/API mode, state-index persistence, cockpit UI work, PR/CI execution, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, or another bootstrap-gap chore.
The implementation does not create RED, implementation, review, landing, or retrospective evidence beyond the current stage until the prior stage gate is satisfied.
Stage 4 review evidence uses pre-PR CodeRabbit and Local Qwen at the current review subject hash unless honest provider refusal or bootstrap-gap evidence is recorded.
Clean-code compliance is evaluated before landing; any accepted non-blocking concern becomes a tagged follow-up or explicit no-action decision.
BANDIT-GAP-OPERATOR-FAIL-CLOSED-BOUNDARY is resolved or explicitly dispositioned in .bandit/bootstrap-gaps.json only after landing action and retrospective closeout evidence exist.

No local server/API mode, cockpit UI implementation, state-index persistence, scheduler execution, claim leases, work surface reservations, worktree lifecycle, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, actor identity policy, PR/CI workflow execution, live reviewer routing change, paid reviewer route, external service integration, installed global skill edit, dependency or lockfile change, or unrelated Phase 8 work is introduced.
verification_plan: Run focused operator-boundary tests for RED/GREEN coverage.
Run node --test test/validate.test.mjs if repo validation behavior is touched.
Run node --test test/cockpit-status.test.mjs if required-operator-input or blocker reporting is touched.
Run node --test test/bootstrap-gaps.test.mjs if bootstrap-gap ledger behavior is touched.

Run npm test if implementation touches shared command routing, validators, policy parsing, cockpit status parsing, bootstrap gaps, review evidence, landing gates, auto-landing policy, or template validation beyond focused tests.
Run npm run bandit -- operator-boundary validate --json if the implementation adds the expected command surface.
Run npm run bandit -- supply-chain-gate validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- risk-classification validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- input-quarantine validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- gaps list.
Run npm run bandit -- auto-land-check <ID> before Stage 5 closeout if auto-landing eligibility behavior is touched.
Run npm run bandit -- coderabbit-review pre-pr <ID> --base origin/main before Stage 4 closeout, unless provider refusal evidence is recorded.
Run npm run bandit -- qwen-review <ID> before Stage 4 closeout.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs review-subject-hash <ID> for aggregate review evidence freshness.
Run npm run bandit -- land-check <ID> before landing.
operator_input_status: No operator-owned input is required before creating this bootstrap-gap chore or writing RED evidence. Repo artifacts identify the gap, source artifacts, rationale, operator-confirmed boundary, existing glossary terms, smell triggers, and Stage 1 rubric requirements. Halt only if implementation would change product direction, UAT policy, workflow policy beyond defining the queued operator fail-closed boundary gap, business tradeoffs, explicit cost/risk posture, external service setup, paid reviewer spend approval, paid reviewer routing, live routing, scheduler authority, claim/worktree authority, installed global skill contents, dependency or lockfile policy, merge/push/deploy authority, or broader workflow scope.
